name: Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit
name_original: Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - trail run circular desde Rupit
region_intro: Jogging around Rupit I Pruit offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its dramatic cliffs, dense forests, and winding rivers, providing a mix of challenging and moderate terrain. Many running routes traverse the iconic Cingles de Rupit, offering varied elevation changes. The area's natural beauty, including waterfalls and historical sites, makes for scenic running trails. Best jogging routes around Rupit I Pruit The most popular jogging route is Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes about 52 minutes to complete, featuring waterfalls and historical points of interest. Another top favourite among local runners is Osca de Cabrera Rocky Ridge – Pla d'Aiats loop from L'Esquirol - Sta. Maria del Roure - Cantonigròs, a difficult 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This route navigates rocky ridges and offers expansive views across the Pla d'Aiats plateau. Local runners also love the Mas Aiats and Cingles d'Aiats – Pla d'Aiats loop from L'Esquirol - Sta. Maria del Roure - Cantonigròs, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Cingles d'Aiats cliffs and the Pla d'Aiats plateau, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Jogging around Rupit I Pruit is defined by dramatic cliffs, forested paths, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging running trails. The routes in Rupit I Pruit are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Rupit I Pruit's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Rupit I Pruit?
A: There are over 100 running routes in Rupit I Pruit, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Yes, Rupit I Pruit offers a few easier running routes. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are at least 2 routes classified as easy, perfect for those new to trail running or looking for a more relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of challenging running trails can I find in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Rupit I Pruit is known for its challenging terrain, with 72 routes classified as difficult. These often feature significant elevation changes and rocky paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24560849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osca de Cabrera Rocky Ridge – Pla d'Aiats loop&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates rocky ridges and offers expansive views.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the Rupit I Pruit area?
A: Yes, many running routes in Rupit I Pruit are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955827597/salto-de-sallent-y-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-trail-run-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24575134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of Rupit – Rupit Village loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic views can I expect on running trails in Rupit I Pruit?
A: The region is characterized by dramatic cliffs, dense forests, and winding rivers. Many trails offer breathtaking views across the Cingles de Rupit and the Pla d'Aiats plateau, providing a truly immersive natural experience.

Q: Are there any historical sites or natural landmarks to see while running?
A: Absolutely. The running routes often traverse areas with historical points of interest and natural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955827597/salto-de-sallent-y-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-trail-run-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues trail&lt;/a&gt; features waterfalls and historical sites, while others pass through the iconic Cingles de Rupit.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Rupit I Pruit?
A: The running trails in Rupit I Pruit are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of challenging and moderate terrain, and the stunning natural beauty, including waterfalls and historical sites.

Q: Are the running trails in Rupit I Pruit suitable for families?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are easier options that families might enjoy, especially those with some hiking experience. Look for the 'easy' or 'moderate' difficulty ratings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24575134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of Rupit – Rupit Village loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate option that could be suitable for active families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Generally, most outdoor trails in natural areas like Rupit I Pruit are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, always check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Rupit I Pruit?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Rupit I Pruit, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially some snow or ice at higher elevations, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955827597/salto-de-sallent-y-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-trail-run-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, featuring impressive waterfalls along its path.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by cafes or places to eat?
A: Many routes start or pass through Rupit village, which has cafes and restaurants. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24575134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of Rupit – Rupit Village loop&lt;/a&gt; begins and ends in Rupit, making it convenient for a post-run refreshment.
tips: Rupit (officially part of Rupit i Pruit municipality) is a village in the county of Osona, in the subregion of the Collsacabra, in Catalonia, Spain. It is located at 822 m above sea level, 98 km from Barcelona.

Una cascada espectacular, sobre una roca igualmente impresionante.

A spectacular waterfall, on an equally impressive rock.
Romanesque church in the municipality of Rupit y Pruit in the region of Osona. It is listed as a protected monument and inventoried in the Catalan Architectural Heritage.
Lookout located at 936m altitude, very close to the anthropomorphic tombs. It has panoramic views of Agullola (921m), Sant Joan de Fàbregues, Salt de Sallent or the cliffs on both sides.
